qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default deployment grants anonymous callers admin access to protected API routes
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets the default `secureEnabled=false` posture and anonymous admin promotion through `verifyGroups()`, while the advisory is the distinct `secureEnabled=true` heartbeat refresh bypass that mints admin JWTs.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Secure mode keeps a predictable JWT signing key and accepts forged admin tokens
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding exploits the predictable fallback secret `frangoteam751` and forged `x-access-token` claims; the advisory instead fixes `/api/heartbeat` re-signing identity from `x-auth-user` without requiring an authenticated request.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated upload route overwrites the active webroot even when secure mode is enabled
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is an unauthenticated file-write/webroot-overwrite bug in `POST /api/upload`; it does not involve the heartbeat refresh endpoint or the token-minting logic fixed by the advisory.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the heartbeat refresh authentication-bypass root cause fixed by GHSA-vwcg-c828-9822. The closest secure-mode finding is a different predictable-secret bug in the same auth subsystem.
evidence: The advisory patch `fe82348d160904d0013b9a3e267d50158f5c7afb` changes only the `/api/heartbeat` refresh flow and `jwt-helper` token-refresh helper so that only authenticated requests can receive a refreshed token.

In the validated FUXA v1.2.9 tree, `server/api/index.js` calls `authJwt.getNewToken(req.headers)` from the `req.body.params` heartbeat branch, and `server/api/jwt-helper.js` signs a fresh token directly from `x-auth-user` header claims.

The `secure-mode-jwt-admin-forgery` qualifying finding instead exploits the predictable JWT secret `frangoteam751` via a forged `x-access-token`; its documented exploit path and reproduced PoC do not use `/api/heartbeat`.
